source: Vibe UI recipes distilled from OpenDesign design-templates and Vibe Gate workflow requirements.

templates.name: Vibe UI commerce home recipe
templates.source: Vibe UI distilled recipe from OpenDesign web-prototype and marketplace/landing patterns
templates.description: A shoppable homepage recipe for ecommerce, marketplace, retail, and lifestyle product stores.
requiredSections: Hero that makes the first viewport unmistakably a shopping experience with product imagery, brand promise, primary shopping CTA, secondary category CTA, and visible product proof.
Category entry section with the required category names, short category descriptors, and stable navigation targets.
Featured product grid with product names, prices, material/spec/use notes, accessible save/cart affordances, and no fake discounts.
Editorial or curator-pick section that explains why a product set belongs together using concrete shopping guidance.
New arrivals, seasonal edit, or collection module with fresh product evidence and a shopping CTA.
Trust, service, curation, material, care, shipping, or return notes grounded in neutral product evidence.
FAQ or objection handling for quality, fit, returns, authenticity, and selection logic.
Final shopping CTA that returns to products, categories, or new arrivals.
sectionIntent: Prove this is a store before the shopper scrolls.
Help shoppers choose a browsing path.
Give enough product evidence to make shopping feel real.
Replace fake reviews with curation logic.
Sustain conversion without SaaS pricing/workflow tropes.
layoutNotes: Give every top-level section a stable data-od-id for review and iteration.
Use DESIGN.md colors, typography, spacing, radius, shadows, density, and interaction rules as the source of truth.
Keep Design Read, dials, TODOs, and internal scaffold text out of the visible UI.
Make section rhythm match the job of each section rather than repeating equal card grids everywhere.
p0SelfCheck: Every visible color is a DESIGN.md token or a documented derivative.
Primary CTAs name the user action rather than using generic labels.
No copied logos, trademarks, proprietary assets, official claims, fake metrics, or placeholder proof remain.
Desktop and mobile layouts have no overlapping text, clipped controls, or horizontal scroll.
All required categories named in the brief appear exactly where required.
Product cards include name, price, and at least one material/spec/use note.
pagePreflight: The hero does not read like SaaS, AI, or consulting; it reads like shopping.
Use product evidence only unless the brief provides real reviews, metrics, or brand relationships.
Do not substitute category names with synonyms when the brief provides exact labels.
Use local, user-owned, or project-owned assets; avoid remote hotlinked stock imagery in final deliverables.

templates.name: Vibe UI product launch recipe
templates.source: Vibe UI distilled recipe from OpenDesign product-launch, waitlist, web-prototype, and saas-landing patterns
templates.description: A launch page recipe for apps, plugins, tools, open-source projects, and new product releases.
requiredSections: Hero with product name, literal offer, launch status, primary try/install/join CTA, secondary proof/demo CTA, and product-state visual.
Problem-to-use-case section that names who the launch is for and what default behavior it replaces.
Feature narrative with 3-5 concrete modules tied to first-use moments.
Demo, install, workflow, or quick-start section with sequential steps and real commands or states when relevant.
Evidence section using changelog, GitHub, docs, integrations, screenshots, benchmarks, or neutral proof categories.
Roadmap, compatibility, or launch notes that set truthful expectations.
FAQ and final CTA for trial, install, waitlist, or docs.
sectionIntent: Give truthful proof without invented traction.
layoutNotes: Give every top-level section a stable data-od-id for review and iteration.
Use DESIGN.md colors, typography, spacing, radius, shadows, density, and interaction rules as the source of truth.
Keep Design Read, dials, TODOs, and internal scaffold text out of the visible UI.
Make section rhythm match the job of each section rather than repeating equal card grids everywhere.
p0SelfCheck: Every visible color is a DESIGN.md token or a documented derivative.
Primary CTAs name the user action rather than using generic labels.
No copied logos, trademarks, proprietary assets, official claims, fake metrics, or placeholder proof remain.
Desktop and mobile layouts have no overlapping text, clipped controls, or horizontal scroll.
pagePreflight: CTA labels must be Try, Install, Join, View demo, Read docs, or another product-specific action.
Do not invent launch traction, customers, stars, funding, or rankings.

templates.name: Vibe UI pricing page recipe
templates.source: Vibe UI distilled recipe from OpenDesign pricing-page
templates.description: A pricing page recipe with plan hierarchy, comparison logic, proof, and objection handling.
requiredSections: Pricing hero with product-specific value, plan framing, and optional monthly/annual toggle.
Plan cards with tier names, prices, positioning, feature bullets, recommended-plan treatment, and direct CTAs.
Feature comparison table grouped by buyer-relevant categories.
Trust, security, billing, or procurement notes using real or neutral evidence.
FAQ for billing, cancellation, limits, teams, support, and enterprise needs.
Final conversion CTA aligned to the recommended plan or sales path.
layoutNotes: Give every top-level section a stable data-od-id for review and iteration.
Use DESIGN.md colors, typography, spacing, radius, shadows, density, and interaction rules as the source of truth.
Keep Design Read, dials, TODOs, and internal scaffold text out of the visible UI.
Make section rhythm match the job of each section rather than repeating equal card grids everywhere.
p0SelfCheck: Every visible color is a DESIGN.md token or a documented derivative.
Primary CTAs name the user action rather than using generic labels.
No copied logos, trademarks, proprietary assets, official claims, fake metrics, or placeholder proof remain.
Desktop and mobile layouts have no overlapping text, clipped controls, or horizontal scroll.
pagePreflight: Prices must be plausible or intentionally omitted with clear contact labels.
Do not use vague feature rows that could apply to any SaaS.

templates.name: Vibe UI waitlist page recipe
templates.source: Vibe UI distilled recipe from OpenDesign waitlist-page
templates.description: A focused pre-launch page recipe for email capture, early access, and beta signup.
requiredSections: Minimal hero with product name, honest launch status, concise offer, and one email/signup CTA.
Short product promise or preview with 2-4 concrete capabilities.
Audience fit or early-access criteria.
Privacy, cadence, or expectation note for what happens after joining.
Optional visual brand moment that follows DESIGN.md without overpowering the form.

templates.name: Vibe UI kanban board recipe
templates.source: Vibe UI distilled recipe from OpenDesign kanban-board
templates.description: A kanban workflow recipe for boards, stages, tasks, owners, and operational state.

Columns with clear stage names, counts, and stable widths.
Task cards with title, owner, status, due/priority metadata, and domain-specific evidence.
Detail, activity, or inspector panel when the brief requires deeper context.
Empty and overflow states for columns.
sectionIntent: Avoid cards that all look identical.

Input, choice, or permission request with clear consequence copy.

Success or next-step state.

templates.description: A profile and portfolio recipe for creators, designers, engineers, writers, and independent builders.

Selected work or case-study section with specific project evidence.
Capability, values, or process section that does not read like generic resume filler.
Writing, talks, experiments, clients, or proof section using real or neutral evidence.
Contact and final CTA.

templates.name: Vibe UI SaaS landing recipe
templates.source: nexu-io/open-design design-templates/saas-landing distilled by Vibe UI
templates.description: A compact recipe for product-led SaaS landing pages with strict section identity, proof, and self-review.
requiredSections: Hero with product name, literal value proposition, one primary CTA, one secondary CTA, and visible product proof.
Credibility strip with real integrations, customers, or neutral proof placeholders that do not invent metrics.
Feature narrative with 3-5 concrete workflow modules instead of generic benefit cards.
Product walkthrough or how-it-works section with sequential steps and UI states.
Use-case or audience section that maps product capability to user roles.
Pricing teaser or conversion block aligned to the selected DESIGN.md rhythm.
FAQ and final CTA using the same visual system as the hero.
layoutNotes: Give every top-level section a stable data-od-id for review and iteration.
Use DESIGN.md colors, typography, spacing, radius, shadows, density, and interaction rules as the source of truth.
Keep Design Read, dials, TODOs, and internal scaffold text out of the visible UI.
Make section rhythm match the job of each section rather than repeating equal card grids everywhere.
p0SelfCheck: Every visible color is a DESIGN.md token or a documented derivative.
Primary CTAs name the user action rather than using generic labels.
No copied logos, trademarks, proprietary assets, official claims, fake metrics, or placeholder proof remain.
Desktop and mobile layouts have no overlapping text, clipped controls, or horizontal scroll.
pagePreflight: Hero names the product and buyer-visible value in the first viewport.
CTA labels are product-specific and not just Get started or Learn more.
Proof strategy is explicit: real evidence, real integrations, product state, or neutral evidence categories.
No visible Design Read, dials, TODO, or internal scaffold text ships in the UI.
Section rhythm is not only repeated equal card grids; each section has a distinct job.
